text:
Pancasiddhantika
(I. 8-10) epoch of 427 Saka year Caitra SuklaPratipady Varahamihira
must have composed it in 505 A.D. This fits in with Al-Birun!'s
statement in 1030 A.D. to the effect that the P.S. was composed 526
years before his time.1 According to Amaraja, Varaha is said to
have died in 587 A.D. It is also held that Varaha visited the Yavana
country, and, according to Persian tradition translated OitPancatantra
into the Pahlavi language at the instance of the then King of
Persia (531 to 576 A.D.)
Al-Biruni and Varahamihira : Al-Blruni (bom in 973 A.D.) came
to India in 1030 A.D. with the object of studying Hindu astrology
and culture. He is also reported to have translated into Arabic the
Brhat Samhitd (B.S.) and the Laghujdtaka (L.J.), some parts of
which are also explained in the pages of his India. Though he has
done a wonderful work, he has committed some errors in interpreting
Varaha5s works.2 He says that the Hindus have no understanding
